contradictions.title: Open ecosystem posture vs closed capability containment
contradictions.current_state: The repo assumes public-facing marketplace and open publication pathways.
contradictions.operator_direction: Keep strongest capability layers private and internal.
contradictions.proposed_resolution: Replace open capability access with bounded products, supervised services, and selective licensing.

contradictions.title: Broad AiDP citizenship vs closed institutional stewardship
contradictions.current_state: AiDP is framed as a broad constitutional AI governance body with phased authority expansion.
contradictions.operator_direction: Atrahasis should remain tightly controlled and not broadly accessible.
contradictions.proposed_resolution: Retain constitutional governance, but narrow operational governance participation to Atrahasis-recognized internal agents and trustees.

contradictions.title: External task marketplace vs negotiated release membrane
contradictions.current_state: Revenue assumes task marketplace, provider onboarding, and enterprise integration as standard channels.
contradictions.operator_direction: Outside parties should negotiate for outcomes, not drive the core system directly.
contradictions.proposed_resolution: Replace marketplace-first economics with consumer utility, enterprise bounded execution, invention brokerage, and selective licensing.

contradictions.title: Founder reward aspiration vs founder non-dynasty rule
contradictions.current_state: The Joshua Dunn Principle prohibits permanent founder governance authority.
contradictions.operator_direction: Joshua Dunn should be rewarded for creating Atrahasis and advancing it.
contradictions.proposed_resolution: Separate founder reward from governance supremacy by using compensation, contracts, or board-approved economic rights rather than constitutional override.

contradictions.title: Frontier bootstrap dependence vs native model sovereignty
contradictions.current_state: Current implementation assumes multiple provider adapters and local model fallback.
contradictions.operator_direction: Atrahasis should eventually use models aligned to AASL and AACP.
contradictions.proposed_resolution: Treat provider APIs as transitional cognition leases while building native control planes, trace corpora, and eventually native models.

contradictions.title: Private control vs public legitimacy
contradictions.current_state: Current AiBC story depends partly on public-benefit transparency and participatory legitimacy.
contradictions.operator_direction: Core capability access should be closed and tightly controlled.
contradictions.proposed_resolution: Adopt open accountability even while keeping capability layers closed.

summary.core_finding: The proposal is internally coherent, but it conflicts with current governance, economics, and publication assumptions strongly enough that it must be treated as a constitutional redesign.
